2605.07793.title: Hybrid TF--IDF Logistic Regression and MLP Neural Baseline for Indonesian Three-Class Sentiment Analysis on Social Media Text
2605.07793.paper_summary: This paper presents a compact three-class sentiment analysis study for Indonesian social media text. The task is formulated with positive, negative, and neutral outputs derived from a fine-grained emotion dataset. The proposed practical baseline combines TF--IDF text features, three lightweight numeric metadata features, and a balanced multinomial Logistic Regression classifier. For comparison, the study also includes a neural baseline using a two-layer multilayer perceptron (MLP) over the same hybrid feature representation. The dataset originally contains 732 rows and 191 fine-grained emotion labels; after cleaning, deduplication, and label remapping, 707 samples remain with an imbalanced distribution of 459 positive, 188 negative, and 60 neutral instances. Experimental results show that the Logistic Regression deployment model reaches 0.8028 accuracy, 0.8003 weighted F1, and 0.7276 macro F1, while project documentation reports a higher-accuracy but non-production MLP baseline. These findings indicate that careful preprocessing, interpretable feature engineering, and class balancing remain competitive for small Indonesian sentiment datasets, whereas the neural baseline is better treated as a comparative experiment than as the default deployment model.
2605.07793.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文提出了一個針對印尼社交媒體文本的緊湊三類情感分析研究。<lb/>該任務的定義包括從精細情感數據集中衍生的正面、負面和中性輸出。<lb/>所提出的實用基準結合了TF--IDF文本特徵、三個輕量級數字元數據特徵以及一個平衡的多項式邏輯回歸分類器。<lb/>為了進行比較，該研究還包括了一個使用兩層多層感知器（MLP）的神經基準，該基準在相同的混合特徵表示上進行。<lb/>該數據集最初包含732行和191個精細情感標籤；經過清理、去重和標籤重新映射後，剩下707個樣本，其中正面、負面和中性實例分別為459、188和60，存在不平衡分佈。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，邏輯回歸部署模型達到0.8028的準確率、0.8003的加權F1和0.7276的宏F1，而項目文檔報告了一個準確率更高但非生產環境的MLP基準。<lb/>這些發現表明，仔細的預處理、可解釋的特徵工程和類別平衡對於小型印尼情感數據集仍然具有競爭力，而神經基準更應被視為比較實驗，而非默認的部署模型。
2605.07793.author: Allya Nurul Islami Pasha et.al.
2605.07793.authors: Allya Nurul Islami Pasha, Eka Fidiya Putri, Luluk Muthoharoh, Ardika Satria, Martin C. T. Manullang
